examples.title: Singly Linked List - Basic Operations
examples.description: Implements fundamental singly linked list operations: Node creation, insertion (at beginning, end, and middle), deletion, traversal, and searching. Essential for understanding linked list fundamentals.
examples.explanation:
1. Node class stores data and pointer to next node
2. SinglyLinkedList class manages the list with head pointer
3. insert_at_beginning: Creates new node, points it to current head, updates head
4. insert_at_end: Traverses to last node, adds new node there
5. delete_node: Searches for node, updates pointers to bypass it
6. search: Traverses list comparing each node's data with key
7. traverse: Collects all data elements into readable string format
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for insert_at_end, delete, search, traverse; O(1) for insert_at_beginning
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for all operations (excluding input storage)

examples.title: Doubly Linked List - Bidirectional Traversal
examples.description: Implements a doubly linked list with nodes having both next and previous pointers. Demonstrates bidirectional traversal, insertion, deletion, and reverse traversal - crucial for understanding advanced data structures like LRU caches.
examples.explanation:
1. DNode has prev and next pointers for bidirectional links
2. insert_at_front: New node points to current head, old head's prev points to new node
3. insert_at_end: New node points to current tail, tail's next points to new node
4. delete_node: Updates both prev and next pointers to maintain doubly links
5. traverse_backward: Starts from tail and follows prev pointers
6. Having both head and tail enables O(1) insertion at both ends
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for insert_at_front, insert_at_end; O(n) for search, delete, traverse

examples.title: Circular Linked List - Round Robin Task Scheduler
examples.description: Implements a circular singly linked list with a practical application: Round-Robin CPU task scheduling algorithm. Demonstrates circular traversal, task management, and time-slice execution - used in operating systems and queue management.
examples.explanation:
1. CNode stores task name, burst time, and next pointer
2. CircularLinkedList: tail.next points to head, creating a circle
3. append: Adds node at end, maintains circular connection
4. remove_task: Handles three cases (head, tail, middle) while preserving circularity
5. round_robin_schedule: Core OS concept - each task gets equal time slice
6. After time slice, moves to next task cyclically until all complete
7. Uses size counter to track remaining tasks in queue
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for append, remove_task; O(n) for full schedule execution
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of tasks
